query: CHP olağanüstü kurultay çağrı heyeti Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi başvuru karar - latest status as of late June / early July 2026. Has Özgür Özel's team filed the application under Civil Code Art 75 requesting appointment of a 3-person çağrı heyeti to convene a CHP extraordinary congress? Has any court issued a decision?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here is a summary of the findings on the CHP extraordinary congress çağrı heyeti court application status:

**Direct answer to the research question:**

1. **Has the application been filed?** — Ambiguous/conflicting. As of late June/early July 2026, Özgür Özel's faction (backed by 833 delegates) had **announced and prepared** the Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi application for a court-appointed **çağrı heyeti**, citing CHP by-laws Art. 48 (and Civil Code Art. 75 principles). Haberler.com and MP Süleyman Bülbül indicated it was being filed **Monday, June 29**, but Hürriyet, Türkiye Gazetesi, and Sözcü (June 29–30) frame it as a still-planned action with **July 11** as the operative deadline. The exact procedural filing status is genuinely reported inconsistently.

2. **Has any court issued a decision?** — **No.** Through July 1, 2026, no Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi decision (granting or refusing a çağrı heyeti) had been reported.

**Key context:**
- Trigger: May 21, 2026 Ankara Regional Court "absolute nullity" (mutlak butlan) ruling on the 38th Congress.
- Signatures delivered to HQ June 17; 10-day leadership deadline expired ~June 26–27 without action.
- Timeline pressure: July 11 (announcement deadline), July 20 (judicial recess), July 26 (last possible congress date; Özel warns of election-eligibility risk).
- Kılıçdaroğlu's camp rejects the extraordinary congress and prefers a September regular congress; Özel's camp has "Plan B" options including a possible new party.

The full structured findings (key facts, 12 sources, and 8 data points) have been recorded via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: CHP'de kurultay krizi büyüyor! Özel'in ekibi bugün mahkemeye gidiyor
sources.snippet: Faction backed by 833 delegates has petitioned the Ankara Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi today for a call committee; if granted, congress required within 45 days.

sources.title: Kılıçdaroğlu harekete geçti! Belediye başkanlarına kötü haber
sources.snippet: Süleyman Bülbül-led faction plans to file at the Asliye/Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi to demand a call committee after HQ refused to process 833 signatures.

sources.title: CHP adliye koridorlarına hapsoldu! Kurultay da ihraçlar da mahkemelik olacak
sources.snippet: Özel faction plans to file with Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi; July 11 deadline (announcement 15 days before July 26 congress); no decision issued yet.

sources.title: Kurultay olmazsa yeni parti
sources.snippet: Özel's team will apply to Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi for a call committee; Plan B includes a new party; judicial recess July 20 could interrupt proceedings.

sources.title: Kurultay kararı yargıya kaldı
sources.snippet: 830 delegate signatures; if HQ does not convene, Özel's team plans lawsuit at Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi; court expected to decide within 15 days, may appoint call committee.

sources.title: Kritik tarih 26 Haziran... Kurultay talebi yargıya taşınacak
sources.snippet: 833 notarized + 170 Istanbul signatures (1,133 total); 10-day window; if not processed, matter goes to civil court which rules within 15 days and may appoint a call committee.

sources.title: Özgür Özel cephesinde 15 günlük süre bitti, gözler Genel Merkez'e çevrildi
sources.snippet: 900 signatures collected; if chairman fails to convene within 45 days, group plans to seek a civil court ruling to force a call committee; Ankara BAM 36th Chamber nullity ruling.

sources.title: CHP'li Emir: Genel Başkan, 45 gün içinde kurultayı toplamak zorunda
sources.snippet: 1,000 delegate file submitted; Art. 48 requires chairman to convene within 45 days; 830 from 37th congress + 170 Istanbul delegates.

sources.title: CHP'de karar haftası
sources.snippet: As of June 29, application not yet filed; Bülbül said 'we will apply to civil court on Monday'; request for çağrı heyeti to compel congress within 45 days.

sources.title: CHP Sözcüsü Zeynel Emre soruları yanıtladı
sources.snippet: July 1: Party Council dropped to 29 members (below required 40); Emre calls for urgent Yargıtay decision; AYM/YSK precedents mandate extraordinary congress if delegate majority signs.

sources.title: Mutlak butlan sonrası CHP kurultayı ceza davası ertelendi
sources.snippet: 6th hearing of congress vote-rigging criminal case held July 1, adjourned to September 16, 2026; 12 defendants; reasoned nullity decision added to file.

sources.title: Olağanüstü kurultay CHP için çözüm mü?
sources.snippet: Legal experts differ; Eminağaoğlu says a call committee could be appointed by a civil court if the general secretary fails to act; Art. 48 mandates 45-day convening.

key_facts: Özgür Özel's faction announced/prepared a Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi (Civil Court of Peace) application seeking a court-appointed 'çağrı heyeti' (convening committee) to force a CHP extraordinary congress, citing CHP by-laws Art. 48 and Civil Code principles (Art. 75).

Sources CONFLICT on whether the petition was formally filed: Haberler.com and MP Süleyman Bülbül indicated filing on Monday June 29, 2026; Hürriyet, Türkiye Gazetesi and Sözcü (June 29-30) describe it as a still-planned/contingent action.
NO court had issued any decision on the çağrı heyeti request as of July 1, 2026.

833 delegates submitted notarized signatures (plus ~170 non-notarized Istanbul delegates, ~1,133 total); delivered to CHP HQ on June 17, 2026 with a 10-day deadline that expired ~June 26-27 without leadership action.
The dispute stems from the May 21, 2026 Ankara Regional Court of Justice (36th Civil Chamber) 'absolute nullity' (mutlak butlan) ruling on the CHP's 38th Ordinary Congress.
Özel's camp emphasizes July 11 as the operative legal deadline (congress announcement required 15 days before the last possible Sunday, July 26).

Özel warned (June 27) the CHP risks being unable to enter elections if no congress is held by July 26; the camp has 'Plan B/C/D' including possibly forming a new party or using shell party Anadolu Birliği Partisi.
Once seized, the court is expected to rule within ~15 days; if granted, the çağrı heyeti must hold the congress within 45 days.
Judicial recess begins July 20, 2026, potentially delaying proceedings; a related nullity appeal is pending at the Yargıtay/İstinaf.
Kılıçdaroğlu's camp rejects the extraordinary congress, favors a regular congress in September, and has launched expulsions (9 MPs to discipline, ~26 provincial chairs removed).
The congress vote-rigging criminal trial (12 defendants incl. Ekrem İmamoğlu) held its 6th hearing July 1, 2026 and was adjourned to September 16, 2026.

data_points.metric: Notarized delegate signatures submitted for extraordinary congress
Total signatures/declarations incl. non-notarized Istanbul delegates
Deadline for leadership to convene congress after signature delivery (party by-law window)
Expected court decision window once petition filed
Maximum period to hold extraordinary congress after valid demand (Art. 48)
Court decisions on çağrı heyeti request issued
CHP Party Council (Parti Meclisi) current members vs required 40
